intro_text: Hi there! Ready to explore the fascinating world of large numbers? 🚀 We'll learn how to read, write, and compare numbers up to crores and billions, making you a number wizard! ✨
complete_message: Awesome job! You've mastered large numbers. Keep up the great work! 🌟

explain_questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 7 in the number 5,73,421?
explain_questions.question_spoken: What is the place value of the digit seven in the number five lakh seventy-three thousand four hundred twenty-one?

steps.s: First, let's look at our number: five lakh seventy-three thousand four hundred twenty-one.
Now, find the digit seven in this number. It's in the second position from the left in the lakhs period.
Counting from the right, the places are Ones, Tens, Hundreds, Thousands, Ten Thousands, Lakhs. So, 7 is in the Ten Thousands place.

explain_questions.confirm_question: What is the place value of 3 in 8,35,102?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: What is the place value of three in eight lakh thirty-five thousand one hundred two?
nudges: Remember, place value tells us the position of a digit in a number.
In the Indian system, numbers are grouped as Ones, Thousands, Lakhs, Crores.
The digit 3 is in the 'thousands' period. What specific place is it in?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q1s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Identify the number: 5,73,421.</text></g><g id="q1s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Locate the digit 7.</text></g><g id="q1s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Determine its place value.</text></g><g id="q1ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Ten thousands</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Write the number 8,45,003 in words using the Indian number system.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Write the number eight lakh forty-five thousand three in words using the Indian number system.

steps.s: First, we group the digits from the right. The Indian system uses commas after the hundreds place, then every two digits. So, it's eight comma forty-five comma zero zero three.
Now, identify the periods. We have the Lakhs period, the Thousands period, and the Ones period.

steps.t: Read each period: Eight lakh, forty-five thousand, three.
steps.s: Combine these to read the number: Eight lakh forty-five thousand three.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Write seven lakh two thousand fifty in words using the Indian system.
nudges: Remember the Indian place value chart: Ones, Tens, Hundreds, Thousands, Ten Thousands, Lakhs, Ten Lakhs, Crores...
Group the digits from the right: first three, then two, then two.
The number is 7,02,050. How do you read the '7' part? How about '02' in the thousands period?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q2s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Group digits using commas: 8,45,003.</text></g><g id="q2s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Identify periods: Lakhs, Thousands, Ones.</text></g><g id="q2s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Read each period: Eight lakh, forty-five thousand, three.</text></g><g id="q2ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Seven lakh two thousand fifty</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Write the number 845,003 in words using the International number system.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Write the number eight hundred forty-five thousand three in words using the International number system.

steps.s: In the International system, we group digits in threes from the right. So, it's eight hundred forty-five comma zero zero three.
Now, identify the periods. We have the Thousands period and the Ones period.

steps.t: Read each period: Eight hundred forty-five thousand, three.
steps.s: Combine these to read the number: Eight hundred forty-five thousand three.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Write seventy million two hundred five thousand one in words using the International system.
answers: Seventy million two hundred five thousand one
nudges: Remember the International place value chart: Ones, Tens, Hundreds, Thousands, Ten Thousands, Hundred Thousands, Millions, Ten Millions...
Group the digits from the right in sets of three.
The number is 70,205,001. How do you read the '70' part in the millions period? And the '205' in the thousands period?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q3s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Group digits using commas: 845,003.</text></g><g id="q3s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Identify periods: Thousands, Ones.</text></g><g id="q3s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Read each period: Eight hundred forty-five thousand, three.</text></g><g id="q3ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">Seventy million two hundred five thousand one</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Write "Two crore five lakh seven thousand" in figures.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Write "Two crore five lakh seven thousand" in figures.

steps.t: Identify periods: Crores, Lakhs, Thousands, Ones.
steps.s: First, let's identify the periods mentioned: Crores, Lakhs, Thousands, and the implied Ones period.

steps.t: Fill in values for each period.
steps.s: We have 'Two crore' (2 in crores place), 'five lakh' (5 in lakhs place), 'seven thousand' (7 in thousands place).

Fill in zeros for any empty places. There are no ten lakhs, ten thousands, hundreds, tens, or ones mentioned. So, it becomes two crore, zero five lakh, zero seven thousand, zero zero zero. This gives us 2,05,07,000.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Write "Fifty-two lakh nine thousand eight hundred" in figures.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Write "Fifty-two lakh nine thousand eight hundred" in figures.
nudges: Start from the largest place value mentioned and work your way down.
Remember to use commas according to the Indian system.
Think about each period: Lakhs, Thousands, Hundreds, Tens, Ones. What number goes in each?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q4s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Identify periods: Crores, Lakhs, Thousands, Ones.</text></g><g id="q4s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Fill in values for each period.</text></g><g id="q4s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Add zeros for missing places: 2,05,07,000.</text></g><g id="q4ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">52,09,800</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Which number is greater: 9,87,65,432 or 9,87,65,321?
explain_questions.question_spoken: Which number is greater: nine crore eighty-seven lakh sixty-five thousand four hundred thirty-two or nine crore eighty-seven lakh sixty-five thousand three hundred twenty-one?

steps.s: Both numbers have 8 digits. This means we need to compare them digit by digit.

steps.t: Compare digits from left to right.
steps.s: Starting from the leftmost digit, both numbers have 9 in the crores place, 8 in the ten lakhs place, 7 in the lakhs place, 6 in the ten thousands place, and 5 in the thousands place. They are the same so far.
The next digit is in the hundreds place. The first number has 4, and the second number has 3. Since 4 is greater than 3, the first number is greater.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Which is smaller: one crore or ninety-nine lakh ninety-nine thousand nine hundred ninety-nine?
nudges: Start by comparing the number of digits. If they are different, the one with more digits is usually larger.
If the number of digits is the same, compare the digits from the leftmost position.
Compare 1,00,00,000 (8 digits) and 99,99,999 (7 digits). The one with fewer digits will be smaller.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q5s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Count the number of digits.</text></g><g id="q5s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Compare digits from left to right.</text></g><g id="q5s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Find the first differing digit.</text></g><g id="q5ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">99,99,999</text></g>

explain_questions.question: How would you write 1 crore in the International number system?
explain_questions.question_spoken: How would you write one crore in the International number system?

steps.s: First, let's write one crore in figures using the Indian system. That's one followed by seven zeros, or one comma zero zero comma zero zero comma zero zero zero.
Now, let's regroup these digits for the International system. We group them in sets of three from the right. So, it becomes ten comma zero zero zero comma zero zero zero.

steps.t: Read the number in International system: 10 million.
steps.s: Reading this, we have ten in the millions period. So, 1 crore is 10 million.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many lakhs are there in 1 million?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many lakhs are there in one million?
nudges: Start by writing 1 million in figures: 1,000,000.
Now, try to read 1,000,000 using the Indian number system grouping.
1,000,000. If you put commas for the Indian system, it becomes 10,00,000. How do you read that?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q6s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Write 1 crore in figures (Indian system): 1,00,00,000.</text></g><g id="q6s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Group digits for International system: 10,000,000.</text></g><g id="q6s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Read the number in International system: 10 million.</text></g><g id="q6ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">10 lakhs</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Arrange the following numbers in ascending order: 5,00,000; 50,000; 5,00,00,000.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Arrange the following numbers in ascending order: five lakh; fifty thousand; five crore.

steps.s: First, let's count the number of digits for each: 5,00,000 has 6 digits; 50,000 has 5 digits; 5,00,00,000 has 8 digits.
The number with the fewest digits is the smallest, and the number with the most digits is the largest. So, 50,000 (5 digits) is smallest, then 5,00,000 (6 digits), then 5,00,00,000 (8 digits).
The ascending order is 50,000; 5,00,000; 5,00,00,000.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Arrange in descending order: one lakh twenty-three thousand four hundred fifty-six; twelve thousand three hundred forty-five; one crore twenty-three lakh forty-five thousand six hundred seventy-eight.
nudges: Descending order means from largest to smallest.
Start by counting the number of digits in each number. More digits usually means a larger number.
The numbers are 1,23,456 (6 digits), 12,345 (5 digits), 1,23,45,678 (8 digits). Which one is the largest?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q7s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Count digits for each number.</text></g><g id="q7s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Order by number of digits (ascending).</text></g><g id="q7s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Final ascending order.</text></g><g id="q7ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">1,23,45,678; 1,23,456; 12,345</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Round 7,48,921 to the nearest lakh.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Round seven lakh forty-eight thousand nine hundred twenty-one to the nearest lakh.

steps.s: First, find the digit in the lakhs place, which is 7.

steps.t: Look at the digit to its right: 4.
steps.s: Now, look at the digit immediately to its right, in the ten thousands place. This digit is 4.
Since 4 is less than 5, we keep the lakh digit as it is and change all digits to its right to zero. So, 7,48,921 rounds down to 7,00,000.

explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Round three lakh eighty-two thousand five hundred to the nearest lakh.
nudges: Identify the digit in the lakhs place.
Look at the digit to the right of the lakhs place (the ten thousands digit).
If the ten thousands digit is 5 or greater, round up the lakhs digit. If it's less than 5, keep the lakhs digit the same. All digits to the right become zero.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q8s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Identify the lakh digit: 7.</text></g><g id="q8s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Look at the digit to its right: 4.</text></g><g id="q8s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Apply rounding rule.</text></g><g id="q8ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">4,00,000</text></g>

explain_questions.question: Estimate the sum of 5,67,890 and 3,21,450 by rounding each number to the nearest ten thousand.
explain_questions.question_spoken: Estimate the sum of five lakh sixty-seven thousand eight hundred ninety and three lakh twenty-one thousand four hundred fifty by rounding each number to the nearest ten thousand.

steps.s: For 5,67,890, the ten thousands digit is 6. The digit to its right is 7 (which is 5 or greater), so we round up. 5,67,890 becomes 5,70,000.
For 3,21,450, the ten thousands digit is 2. The digit to its right is 1 (which is less than 5), so we keep it as is. 3,21,450 becomes 3,20,000.
Now, add the rounded numbers: 5,70,000 + 3,20,000 = 8,90,000. So, the estimated sum is eight lakh ninety thousand.

explain_questions.confirm_question: Estimate the difference between 9,12,345 and 4,87,654 by rounding to the nearest lakh.
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: Estimate the difference between nine lakh twelve thousand three hundred forty-five and four lakh eighty-seven thousand six hundred fifty-four by rounding to the nearest lakh.
nudges: First, round each number to the nearest lakh.
For 9,12,345, the lakhs digit is 9. The digit to its right is 1. What happens?
For 4,87,654, the lakhs digit is 4. The digit to its right is 8. What happens? Then subtract the rounded numbers.
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q9s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Round 5,67,890 to nearest ten thousand.</text></g><g id="q9s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Round 3,21,450 to nearest ten thousand.</text></g><g id="q9s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Add the rounded numbers.</text></g><g id="q9ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">4,00,000</text></g>

explain_questions.question: If a country's population is 1.2 billion, how many crores is that in the Indian number system?
explain_questions.question_spoken: If a country's population is one point two billion, how many crores is that in the Indian number system?

steps.s: First, let's write 1.2 billion in figures in the International system. 1 billion is 1,000,000,000. So, 1.2 billion is 1,200,000,000.
Now, let's regroup this number using the Indian system's commas: 1,20,00,00,000. This is one hundred twenty crore.
So, 1.2 billion is equal to 120 crores.

explain_questions.confirm_question: How many millions are there in 50 lakhs?
explain_questions.confirm_question_spoken: How many millions are there in fifty lakhs?
nudges: Start by writing 50 lakhs in figures using the Indian system.
Now, convert that number to the International system grouping.
50 lakhs is 50,00,000. How do you read 5,000,000 in the International system?
explain_questions.anim_svg: <g id="q10s0" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="28" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Convert 1.2 billion to figures (International).</text></g><g id="q10s1" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="68" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">Convert to Indian system grouping.</text></g><g id="q10s2" opacity="0"><text x="22" y="108" font-size="11" font-weight="800" fill="#5a4a30">State the equivalent in crores.</text></g><g id="q10ans" opacity="0"><rect x="40" y="148" width="320" height="24" rx="7" fill="#eef2eb" stroke="#6b4c2a" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="210" y="163" text-anchor="middle" font-family="Share Tech Mono" font-size="12" font-weight="bold" fill="#7a8c6e">5 millions</text></g>

practice_questions.question: What is the place value of the digit 9 in 9,00,00,000?
practice_questions.question_spoken: What is the place value of the digit nine in nine crore?
steps: Identify the digit 9 in the number 9,00,00,000.
Starting from the right, count the place values: Ones, Tens, Hundreds, Thousands, Ten Thousands, Lakhs, Ten Lakhs, Crores.
The digit 9 is in the Crores place.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: For numbers like 9,00,00,000, the place value of the first digit is simply the name of the period it falls into, as all other digits are zero!
practice_questions.alt: You can also write the number in an expanded form. 9,00,00,000 = 9 x 1,00,00,000. The number 1,00,00,000 represents one crore, so the place value of 9 is crores.

practice_questions.question: Write 7,03,05,001 in words using the Indian number system.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Write seven crore three lakh five thousand one in words using the Indian number system.
answers: Seven crore three lakh five thousand one
steps: Group the digits using Indian system commas: 7,03,05,001.
Identify the periods: 7 is in the Crores period, 03 is in the Lakhs period, 05 is in the Thousands period, and 001 is in the Ones period.
Read each period: Seven crore, three lakh, five thousand, one.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Always start reading from the leftmost period. If a period has only zeros, you can skip saying its name, like 'zero lakh' is just skipped.
practice_questions.alt: You can create a place value chart for the Indian system and fill in the digits. Then, read the number by looking at the value in each period (Crores, Lakhs, Thousands, Ones).

practice_questions.question: Write 70,305,001 in words using the International number system.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Write seventy million three hundred five thousand one in words using the International number system.
answers: Seventy million three hundred five thousand one
steps: Group the digits using International system commas: 70,305,001.
Identify the periods: 70 is in the Millions period, 305 is in the Thousands period, and 001 is in the Ones period.
Read each period: Seventy million, three hundred five thousand, one.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: In the International system, each comma separates a 'family' of three digits (thousands, millions, billions). Just read the number in each family and add the family name!
practice_questions.alt: Mentally (or physically) draw an International place value chart. Place the digits 70,305,001 into it. Then, read the number from left to right, stating the value of each group of three digits and its corresponding period name.

practice_questions.question: Write "Fifty-two lakh nine thousand eight hundred" in figures.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Write "Fifty-two lakh nine thousand eight hundred" in figures.
steps: Identify the largest period: Fifty-two lakh (52,00,000).
Identify the thousands period: nine thousand (09,000).
Identify the hundreds, tens, ones: eight hundred (800). Combine them: 52,09,800.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: For each period (Lakhs, Thousands), make sure you have two digits. If it's 'nine thousand', write '09'. If it's 'five lakh', write '05'. This helps avoid missing zeros!
practice_questions.alt: Start by writing the number for each period separately: 52 lakh = 52,00,000. Nine thousand = 0,09,000. Eight hundred = 0,00,800. Then, add these numbers together: 52,00,000 + 9,000 + 800 = 52,09,800.

practice_questions.question: What is the smallest 7-digit number?
practice_questions.question_spoken: What is the smallest seven-digit number?
steps: A 7-digit number means it has digits from the lakhs period down to the ones period.
To make it the smallest, the leftmost digit must be the smallest possible non-zero digit, which is 1.
All other digits must be 0s. So, it's 1 followed by six 0s: 10,00,000.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: The smallest N-digit number is always 1 followed by N-1 zeros. For 7 digits, it's 1 followed by 6 zeros.
practice_questions.alt: Think of the largest 6-digit number, which is 9,99,999. The smallest 7-digit number is simply the successor of the largest 6-digit number. Add 1 to 9,99,999 to get 10,00,000.

practice_questions.question: What is the successor of 99,99,999?
practice_questions.question_spoken: What is the successor of ninety-nine lakh ninety-nine thousand nine hundred ninety-nine?
steps: The successor of a number is the number that comes immediately after it.
To find the successor, add 1 to the given number.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When you add 1 to a number ending in all 9s, all the 9s become 0s, and the digit to their left increases by 1 (or a new 1 appears if it's all 9s).
practice_questions.alt: Imagine a number line. The successor is the next whole number. If you have 99,99,999, adding one pushes it over to the next major milestone, which is 1 crore.

practice_questions.question: Which is smaller: 1,00,00,000 or 99,99,999?
practice_questions.question_spoken: Which is smaller: one crore or ninety-nine lakh ninety-nine thousand nine hundred ninety-nine?
steps: Count the number of digits in each number. 1,00,00,000 has 8 digits. 99,99,999 has 7 digits.
A number with fewer digits is generally smaller than a number with more digits.
Since 99,99,999 has 7 digits and 1,00,00,000 has 8 digits, 99,99,999 is smaller.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When comparing numbers, always check the number of digits first! It's the quickest way to find the smaller or larger number if the digit counts are different.
practice_questions.alt: You can think of it as comparing 1 crore with 'almost 1 crore'. 99,99,999 is just one less than 1 crore. Therefore, 99,99,999 is smaller.

practice_questions.question: Arrange the following numbers in descending order: 1,23,456; 12,345; 1,23,45,678.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Arrange the following numbers in descending order: one lakh twenty-three thousand four hundred fifty-six; twelve thousand three hundred forty-five; one crore twenty-three lakh forty-five thousand six hundred seventy-eight.
steps: Count the number of digits for each: 1,23,456 (6 digits); 12,345 (5 digits); 1,23,45,678 (8 digits).
Descending order means largest to smallest. The number with the most digits is largest: 1,23,45,678.
Then comes 1,23,456, and finally the smallest, 12,345. So, the order is 1,23,45,678; 1,23,456; 12,345.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When ordering, first sort by the number of digits. More digits = larger number. If digit counts are the same, then compare from left to right.
practice_questions.alt: You can convert all numbers to the same system (e.g., International) if that helps you visualize. 1,23,456; 12,345; 12,345,678. Then compare them directly.

practice_questions.question: How many lakhs are there in 1 million?
practice_questions.question_spoken: How many lakhs are there in one million?
steps: Write 1 million in figures (International system): 1,000,000.
Now, apply the Indian system's comma grouping to this number: 10,00,000.
Read the number using the Indian system: Ten lakh. So, there are 10 lakhs in 1 million.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: Remember that 100 thousand is 1 lakh, and 10 lakh is 1 million. This 10x relationship is key!
practice_questions.alt: You can also think of it as a ratio. 1 million = 1000 thousands. 1 lakh = 100 thousands. So, 1 million / 1 lakh = (1000 thousands) / (100 thousands) = 10. Thus, 1 million is 10 lakhs.

practice_questions.question: Round 4,56,78,901 to the nearest ten lakh.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Round four crore fifty-six lakh seventy-eight thousand nine hundred one to the nearest ten lakh.
steps: Identify the ten lakhs digit: 5 in 4,56,78,901.
Look at the digit to its right (lakhs place): 6. Since 6 is 5 or greater, we round up the ten lakhs digit.
The 5 becomes 6, and all digits to its right become 0s. So, 4,56,78,901 rounds to 4,60,00,000.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When rounding to a specific place, focus only on that digit and the one immediately to its right. The rest of the number to the left stays the same, and to the right becomes zeros.
practice_questions.alt: Consider the two nearest ten lakh multiples: 4,50,00,000 and 4,60,00,000. The number 4,56,78,901 is closer to 4,60,00,000 than to 4,50,00,000 because 56 lakh is more than halfway past 50 lakh towards 60 lakh.

practice_questions.question: Form the largest 8-digit number using digits 3,0,7,1,9,2,5,8 without repetition.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Form the largest eight-digit number using digits three, zero, seven, one, nine, two, five, eight without repetition.
steps: To form the largest number, arrange the given digits in descending order.
The digits are 9, 8, 7, 5, 3, 2, 1, 0.
Place them in order: 98753210. Add commas for readability: 9,87,53,210.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: For the largest number, always put the biggest digits in the highest place value positions (leftmost). For the smallest, put the smallest digits in the highest place value positions (but never 0 as the first digit!).
practice_questions.alt: You can think of it as filling 8 empty slots from left to right. For the first slot (crores place), pick the largest available digit. For the second slot (ten lakhs place), pick the next largest, and so on, until all slots are filled.

practice_questions.question: Find the difference between the place values of the two 5s in 5,25,34,100.
practice_questions.question_spoken: Find the difference between the place values of the two fives in five crore twenty-five lakh thirty-four thousand one hundred.
steps: Identify the place value of the first 5 (leftmost): It's in the Crores place, so its value is 5,00,00,000.
Identify the place value of the second 5: It's in the Lakhs place, so its value is 5,00,000.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When finding the difference in place values, write out the full place value for each digit, then subtract. Don't just subtract the digits themselves!
practice_questions.alt: You can visualize the number as 5 crores and 25 lakhs. The first 5 is 5 crores. The second 5 is 5 lakhs. The difference is 5 crores - 5 lakhs. Since 1 crore = 100 lakhs, 5 crores = 500 lakhs. So, 500 lakhs - 5 lakhs = 495 lakhs, which is 4,95,00,000.

practice_questions.question: A factory produced 2,35,467 toys in January and 1,87,954 toys in February. How many toys were produced in total?
practice_questions.question_spoken: A factory produced two lakh thirty-five thousand four hundred sixty-seven toys in January and one lakh eighty-seven thousand nine hundred fifty-four toys in February. How many toys were produced in total?
steps: To find the total, we need to add the number of toys produced in January and February.
Add the numbers: 2,35,467 + 1,87,954.
Performing the addition: 2,35,467 + 1,87,954 = 4,23,421.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When adding large numbers, align them vertically by place value. Start adding from the rightmost digit (ones place) and carry over when needed. This prevents errors!
practice_questions.alt: You can estimate the sum first to get a rough idea. Rounding to the nearest lakh: 2,00,000 + 2,00,000 = 4,00,000. This tells you the answer should be around 4 lakh. Then do the exact calculation.

practice_questions.question: A school library has 1,23,456 books. If 78,901 books are issued, estimate the remaining books to the nearest thousand.
practice_questions.question_spoken: A school library has one lakh twenty-three thousand four hundred fifty-six books. If seventy-eight thousand nine hundred one books are issued, estimate the remaining books to the nearest thousand.
steps: Round 1,23,456 to the nearest thousand: The thousands digit is 3. The digit to its right is 4 (less than 5), so it rounds to 1,23,000.
Round 78,901 to the nearest thousand: The thousands digit is 8. The digit to its right is 9 (5 or greater), so it rounds to 79,000.
Subtract the rounded numbers: 1,23,000 - 79,000 = 44,000.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: When estimating, always round *before* performing the operation (addition, subtraction, etc.). This is the essence of estimation!
practice_questions.alt: First, find the exact difference: 1,23,456 - 78,901 = 44,555. Then, round this exact answer to the nearest thousand. The thousands digit is 4. The digit to its right is 5, so round up. 44,555 rounds to 45,000. (Note: The question asked to round *each number* first, so the primary answer is 44,000. This alt method is for rounding the final exact answer).

practice_questions.question: The distance from Earth to Moon is approximately 3,84,400 km. Write this distance in words in the International system.
practice_questions.question_spoken: The distance from Earth to Moon is approximately three lakh eighty-four thousand four hundred kilometers. Write this distance in words in the International system.
answers: Three hundred eighty-four thousand four hundred kilometers
Three hundred eighty-four thousand four hundred km
steps: Take the number: 3,84,400.
Regroup the digits for the International system: 384,400.
Read the number in International system: Three hundred eighty-four thousand four hundred.
practice_questions.trick: Rishika Trick: To convert from Indian to International, just remove the Indian commas and re-insert International commas (every three digits from the right). Then read!
practice_questions.alt: You can think of 3,84,400 as 3 lakh 84 thousand 4 hundred. In the International system, 1 lakh is 100 thousand. So, 3 lakh is 300 thousand. Adding the 84 thousand, we get 384 thousand. Then add the 4 hundred. This gives Three hundred eighty-four thousand four hundred.
